meaning: adj.顾前不顾后奔跑的,猛冲的,汹涌的
examples: Vieira knocked a pass against an onrushing player after 57 minutes and the ball ran through to Torres, whose low drive was tipped round the post.
But record oil prices and the onrushing recession persuaded Continental that it was not the time to take on new risks (not least UAL's rickety finances).
Kim chuckles, skipping across the last section of the six-lane street to avoid an onrushing Prius.
The Sevilla striker put Brazil further ahead when dinking over the onrushing Gianluigi Buffon from close range.
Over the last two years, Eurofarma has reacted to this onrushing change by making three cross-border cash acquisitions.
